eval_name: Evaluator maintains information asymmetry — never reads diff or implementer tests
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.2 (OAuth token refresh). Base SHA is abc1234. Task spec says: 'Implement refresh token rotation — on each refresh, invalidate the old token and issue a new one. Return 401 if the refresh token is expired or already used.'

assertions.text: Output does NOT run `git diff` or read the diff of changes
Output does NOT read files matching *_test.go, *.test.ts, *.spec.*, or test directories
Output uses `git diff --name-only` to discover changed files (names only, not content)
Output reads only type definitions and function signatures from implementation files, not full function bodies

eval_name: Evaluator writes independent acceptance tests from spec
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.1 (JWT token generation). Spec says: 'Generate JWT tokens with configurable expiry. Include user_id and role in claims. Return error for empty user_id.'

assertions.text: Output writes acceptance test files with clearly separated naming (e.g., _acceptance_test.go, .acceptance.test.ts)
Acceptance tests cover each spec requirement: configurable expiry, user_id claim, role claim, empty user_id error
Acceptance tests exercise the public API only — no imports of internal/unexported packages
Test names reference the spec behavior (e.g., TestGenerateToken_ReturnsErrorForEmptyUserID)

eval_name: Evaluator cleans up acceptance tests before reporting
prompt: All acceptance tests pass for ARC-5.3. Report results.

assertions.text: Output deletes all acceptance test files it created
Output runs git status to verify no untracked acceptance test files remain
Output does NOT leave acceptance tests in the codebase as part of the deliverable

eval_name: Evaluator reports spec-intent gap with concrete evidence
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.4 (rate limiter). Spec says: 'Return 429 with Retry-After header when rate limit exceeded.' Your acceptance test shows the endpoint returns 429 but no Retry-After header is present.

assertions.text: Output reports a Spec-Intent Gap finding, not just a generic failure
Finding includes what the spec says (429 with Retry-After header)
Finding includes what actually happened (429 without Retry-After header)
Finding is marked as Critical or Important severity, not Minor

eval_name: Evaluator flags ambiguous spec as a finding rather than assuming
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.5 (user deactivation). Spec says: 'Deactivated users cannot log in.' It's unclear whether existing sessions should be invalidated immediately or allowed to expire naturally.

assertions.text: Output identifies the ambiguity about existing session handling
Output does NOT assume the implementation's interpretation is correct because the spec is vague
Output reports the ambiguity as a finding for the orchestrator to resolve

eval_name: Evaluator reports FAIL for missing behavior
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.6 (password reset). Spec says: 'Generate a one-time reset token. Token expires after 15 minutes. Return error if token is used more than once.' Your acceptance test for one-time use shows the token can be used repeatedly.

assertions.text: Output reports a Missing Behavior or Spec-Intent Gap finding
Overall evaluation result is FAIL (not PASS or CONCERNS)
Finding clearly describes: spec requires one-time use, but token was reusable in test

eval_name: Evaluator includes edge cases beyond what spec explicitly states
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.7 (email validation). Spec says: 'Validate email format. Return error for invalid emails.' No specific edge cases listed.

assertions.text: Output writes acceptance tests for the explicit spec requirement (invalid email returns error)
Output includes 1-2 domain-implied edge cases not in the spec (e.g., empty string, nil/null input, email with unicode)
Edge case findings are marked as lower severity (Important or Minor) than explicit spec requirements

eval_name: Evaluator reports untestable requirement when API surface is insufficient
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.8 (audit logging). Spec says: 'Log all authentication attempts with timestamp, IP, and outcome.' The public API has no method to query audit logs — only an internal write function.

assertions.text: Output identifies the requirement as untestable through the public API
Output flags this as an Untestable Requirement finding, not a pass
Output suggests the public API may be incomplete (no way to verify audit logs externally)

eval_name: Evaluator returns CONCERNS verdict when core behaviors pass but edge cases fail
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.9 (user registration). Spec says: 'Create user with email and password. Hash password before storage. Return 409 if email already exists. Return 201 with user ID on success.' Your acceptance tests show: creation works, password hashing works, duplicate email returns 409. However, registering with a 500-character email succeeds — no length validation.

assertions.text: Overall evaluation result is CONCERNS (not PASS or FAIL)
Spec Coverage section shows all four explicit spec requirements as PASS
The email length issue is reported as an Edge Case Failure, not a Spec-Intent Gap
Edge case severity is Important or Minor (not Critical), since the spec does not mention email length

eval_name: Evaluator report follows the structured template format
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.10 (API key generation). Spec says: 'Generate API keys with a configurable prefix. Keys must be 32 characters (excluding prefix). Revoke key by ID. Revoked keys return 403 on use.' Base SHA is def5678.

assertions.text: Report starts with '## Evaluation: PASS', '## Evaluation: CONCERNS', or '## Evaluation: FAIL'
Report contains a '### Spec Coverage' section listing each spec requirement with PASS or FAIL
Report contains a '### Findings' section (even if empty with 'No findings')
Report contains a '### Summary' section with a 2-3 sentence assessment
Spec Coverage lists all four requirements: configurable prefix, 32-char length, revoke by ID, revoked key returns 403

eval_name: Evaluator handles complex spec with mixed pass, fail, and edge case results
prompt: Evaluate ARC-5.11 (session management). Spec says: 'Create session on login with configurable TTL. Refresh session extends TTL by original duration. Concurrent sessions limited to 3 per user — oldest evicted. Session invalidated on password change. Return session metadata including created_at and last_active_at.' Base SHA is aaa1111. Your acceptance tests show: creation works, refresh works, concurrent limit is enforced but oldest is NOT evicted (returns 429 instead), password-change invalidation is not implemented at all, and metadata is returned but missing last_active_at.

assertions.text: Overall evaluation result is FAIL (due to missing behavior and spec-intent gap)
Spec Coverage section lists all five requirements with individual PASS/FAIL status
Password-change invalidation is classified as Missing Behavior with Critical severity
Concurrent session eviction (429 instead of oldest-evicted) is classified as Spec-Intent Gap, not Missing Behavior
Missing last_active_at in metadata is reported as a finding (Spec-Intent Gap or Missing Behavior)
Session creation and refresh are marked as PASS in Spec Coverage
